verses.kjv: And it came to pass at that time, that Judah went down from his brethren, and turned in to a certain Adullamite, whose name was Hirah.
verses.ct: Around that time, Judah left his brothers and went to stay with a man from Adullam named Hirah.
verses.web: At that time, Judah went down from his brothers, and visited a certain Adullamite, whose name was Hirah.

verses.kjv: And Judah saw there a daughter of a certain Canaanite, whose name was Shuah; and he took her, and went in unto her.
verses.ct: While there, Judah noticed the daughter of a Canaanite man named Shuah. He married her and slept with her.
verses.web: There, Judah saw the daughter of a certain Canaanite man named Shua. He took her, and went in to her.

verses.kjv: And she conceived, and bare a son; and he called his name Er.
verses.ct: She became pregnant and gave birth to a son, and he named the boy Er.
verses.web: She conceived, and bore a son; and he named him Er.

verses.kjv: And she conceived again, and bare a son; and she called his name Onan.
verses.ct: She became pregnant again and gave birth to another son, and she named him Onan.
verses.web: She conceived again, and bore a son; and she named him Onan.

verses.kjv: And she yet again conceived, and bare a son; and called his name Shelah: and he was at Chezib, when she bare him.
verses.ct: She conceived once more and gave birth to yet another son, and she named him Shelah. Judah was at Chezib when this child was born.
verses.web: She yet again bore a son, and named him Shelah. He was at Chezib when she bore him.

verses.kjv: And Judah took a wife for Er his firstborn, whose name was Tamar.
verses.ct: Judah chose a wife for his firstborn Er, and her name was Tamar.
verses.web: Judah took a wife for Er, his firstborn, and her name was Tamar.

verses.kjv: And Er, Judah’s firstborn, was wicked in the sight of the LORD; and the LORD slew him.
verses.ct: But Er, Judah's firstborn, was evil in the LORD's sight, so the LORD put him to death.
verses.web: Er, Judah’s firstborn, was wicked in LORD’s sight. So LORD killed him.

verses.kjv: And Judah said unto Onan, Go in unto thy brother’s wife, and marry her, and raise up seed to thy brother.
verses.ct: Judah told Onan, "Sleep with your brother's wife and fulfill your duty to her as a brother-in-law, so that your brother may have offspring."
verses.web: Judah said to Onan, “Go in to your brother’s wife, and perform the duty of a husband’s brother to her, and raise up offspring for your brother.”

verses.kjv: And Onan knew that the seed should not be his; and it came to pass, when he went in unto his brother’s wife, that he spilled it on the ground, lest that he should give seed to his brother.
verses.ct: Onan understood that the children would not be considered his own. So whenever he slept with his brother's wife, he let his semen fall on the ground to avoid producing offspring for his brother.
verses.web: Onan knew that the offspring wouldn’t be his; and when he went in to his brother’s wife, he spilled his semen on the ground, lest he should give offspring to his brother.

verses.kjv: And the thing which he did displeased the LORD: wherefore he slew him also.
verses.ct: What Onan did was evil in the LORD's sight, so the LORD put him to death as well.
verses.web: The thing which he did was evil in LORD’s sight, and he killed him also.

verses.kjv: Then said Judah to Tamar his daughter in law, Remain a widow at thy father’s house, till Shelah my son be grown: for he said, Lest peradventure he die also, as his brethren did. And Tamar went and dwelt in her father’s house.
verses.ct: Judah then told his daughter-in-law Tamar, "Go back to your father's home and live there as a widow until my son Shelah is old enough." He said this because he was afraid Shelah might also die like his brothers. So Tamar went to live in her father's household.
verses.web: Then Judah said to Tamar, his daughter-in-law, “Remain a widow in your father’s house, until Shelah, my son, is grown up;” for he said, “Lest he also die, like his brothers.” Tamar went and lived in her father’s house.

verses.kjv: And in process of time the daughter of Shuah Judah’s wife died; and Judah was comforted, and went up unto his sheepshearers to Timnath, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite.
verses.ct: After a long time had passed, Judah's wife, the daughter of Shuah, died. Once Judah had finished mourning, he traveled up to Timnath with his friend Hirah the Adullamite to be with his sheepshearers.
verses.web: After many days, Shua’s daughter, the wife of Judah, died. Judah was comforted, and went up to his sheep shearers to Timnah, he and his friend Hirah, the Adullamite.

verses.kjv: And it was told Tamar, saying, Behold thy father in law goeth up to Timnath to shear his sheep.
verses.ct: Someone informed Tamar, "Your father-in-law is heading to Timnath to shear his sheep."
verses.web: Tamar was told, “Behold, your father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep.”

verses.kjv: And she put her widow’s garments off from her, and covered her with a vail, and wrapped herself, and sat in an open place, which is by the way to Timnath; for she saw that Shelah was grown, and she was not given unto him to wife.
verses.ct: So she removed her widow's clothing, covered herself with a veil to disguise her appearance, and sat down at the entrance to Enaim, which is on the road to Timnath. She did this because she realized that Shelah had grown up, yet she had not been given to him as a wife.
verses.web: She took off the garments of her widowhood, and covered herself with her veil, and wrapped herself, and sat in the gate of Enaim, which is on the way to Timnah; for she saw that Shelah was grown up, and she wasn’t given to him as a wife.

verses.kjv: When Judah saw her, he thought her to be an harlot; because she had covered her face.
verses.ct: Judah spotted her and assumed she was a prostitute, since her face was hidden.
verses.web: When Judah saw her, he thought that she was a prostitute, for she had covered her face.

verses.kjv: And he turned unto her by the way, and said, Go to, I pray thee, let me come in unto thee; (for he knew not that she was his daughter in law.) And she said, What wilt thou give me, that thou mayest come in unto me?
verses.ct: He approached her beside the road and said, "Come now, let me sleep with you." He had no idea she was his daughter-in-law. She replied, "What will you pay me to sleep with me?"
verses.web: He turned to her by the way, and said, “Please come, let me come in to you,” for he didn’t know that she was his daughter-in-law. She said, “What will you give me, that you may come in to me?”

verses.kjv: And he said, I will send thee a kid from the flock. And she said, Wilt thou give me a pledge, till thou send it?
verses.ct: He answered, "I'll send you a young goat from my flock." She asked, "Will you give me something as a guarantee until you send it?"
verses.web: He said, “I will send you a young goat from the flock.” She said, “Will you give me a pledge, until you send it?”

verses.kjv: And he said, What pledge shall I give thee? And she said, Thy signet, and thy bracelets, and thy staff that is in thine hand. And he gave it her, and came in unto her, and she conceived by him.
verses.ct: He asked, "What kind of guarantee do you want?" She replied, "Your seal, your cord, and the staff you're carrying." So he handed them over, slept with her, and she became pregnant by him.
verses.web: He said, “What pledge will I give you?” She said, “Your signet and your cord, and your staff that is in your hand.” He gave them to her, and came in to her, and she conceived by him.

verses.kjv: And she arose, and went away, and laid by her vail from her, and put on the garments of her widowhood.
verses.ct: She got up and left, then took off her veil and dressed again in her widow's clothing.
verses.web: She arose, and went away, and put off her veil from her, and put on the garments of her widowhood.

verses.kjv: And Judah sent the kid by the hand of his friend the Adullamite, to receive his pledge from the woman’s hand: but he found her not.
verses.ct: Judah sent his friend the Adullamite to deliver the young goat and retrieve the items he had left as a guarantee, but Hirah could not locate the woman.
verses.web: Judah sent the young goat by the hand of his friend, the Adullamite, to receive the pledge from the woman’s hand, but he didn’t find her.

verses.kjv: Then he asked the men of that place, saying, Where is the harlot, that was openly by the way side? And they said, There was no harlot in this place.
verses.ct: He asked the local men, "Where is the prostitute who was sitting out in the open along the road?" They answered, "No prostitute has been here."
verses.web: Then he asked the men of her place, saying, “Where is the prostitute, that was at Enaim by the road?” They said, “There has been no prostitute here.”

verses.kjv: And he returned to Judah, and said, I cannot find her; and also the men of the place said, that there was no harlot in this place.
verses.ct: So he went back to Judah and reported, "I wasn't able to find her. The local men even said that no prostitute had been in that area."
verses.web: He returned to Judah, and said, “I haven’t found her; and also the men of the place said, ‘There has been no prostitute here.’ ”

verses.kjv: And Judah said, Let her take it to her, lest we be shamed: behold, I sent this kid, and thou hast not found her.
verses.ct: Judah responded, "Let her keep the items, otherwise we'll become a laughingstock. After all, I did send the young goat, but you couldn't find her."
verses.web: Judah said, “Let her keep it, lest we be shamed. Behold, I sent this young goat, and you haven’t found her.”

verses.kjv: And it came to pass about three months after, that it was told Judah, saying, Tamar thy daughter in law hath played the harlot; and also, behold, she is with child by whoredom. And Judah said, Bring her forth, and let her be burnt.
verses.ct: About three months later, Judah received word: "Your daughter-in-law Tamar has acted like a prostitute, and now she is pregnant because of it." Judah demanded, "Bring her out and have her burned to death!"
verses.web: About three months later, Judah was told, “Tamar, your daughter-in-law, has played the prostitute. Moreover, behold, she is with child by prostitution.” Judah said, “Bring her out, and let her be burned.”

verses.kjv: When she was brought forth, she sent to her father in law, saying, By the man, whose these are, am I with child: and she said, Discern, I pray thee, whose are these, the signet, and bracelets, and staff.
verses.ct: As she was being brought out, she sent a message to her father-in-law: "The man who owns these things is the one who made me pregnant." She added, "Please examine these and identify whose seal, cord, and staff these are."
verses.web: When she was brought out, she sent to her father-in-law, saying, “I am with child by the man who owns these.” She also said, “Please discern whose these are—the signet, and the cords, and the staff.”

verses.kjv: And Judah acknowledged them, and said, She hath been more righteous than I; because that I gave her not to Shelah my son. And he knew her again no more.
verses.ct: Judah recognized them and declared, "She is more righteous than I am, because I refused to give her to my son Shelah." He never slept with her again after that.
verses.web: Judah acknowledged them, and said, “She is more righteous than I, because I didn’t give her to Shelah, my son.” He knew her again no more.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass in the time of her travail, that, behold, twins were in her womb.
verses.ct: When the time came for her to deliver, it turned out there were twins in her womb.
verses.web: In the time of her travail, behold, twins were in her womb.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when she travailed, that the one put out his hand: and the midwife took and bound upon his hand a scarlet thread, saying, This came out first.
verses.ct: During the birth, one baby extended his hand out first. The midwife grabbed it and tied a scarlet thread around his wrist, saying, "This one appeared first."
verses.web: When she travailed, one put out a hand, and the midwife took and tied a scarlet thread on his hand, saying, “This came out first.”

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as he drew back his hand, that, behold, his brother came out: and she said, How hast thou broken forth? this breach be upon thee: therefore his name was called Pharez.
verses.ct: But then he pulled his hand back, and his brother came out instead. The midwife exclaimed, "What a breach you have forced!" So he was named Pharez.
verses.web: As he drew back his hand, behold, his brother came out, and she said, “Why have you made a breach for yourself?” Therefore his name was called Perez.

verses.kjv: And afterward came out his brother, that had the scarlet thread upon his hand: and his name was called Zarah.
verses.ct: After that, his brother with the scarlet thread on his wrist was born, and he was named Zarah.
verses.web: Afterward his brother came out, who had the scarlet thread on his hand, and his name was called Zerah.
